title: Hello I need the answers for the below comprehensive exam questions Many businesses face huge losses due to the current …

turns.speaker: user
turns.text:
Hello I need the answers for the below comprehensive exam questions Many businesses face huge losses due to the current environment specially those who did not analyse the situation and changing trends before time but some
  businesses who did analysis and rapidly adopted the changes demanded by the situation saved them to face the loss. Other than the pandemic there are multiple challenges that the companies are facing while conducting their operations such as, uncertainty about the future, management of financial issues, inflation, climate change, use of new technologies, new business techniques, competitors in the market, meeting the customer’s requirements criteria, monitoring the business activities and many more.
Like all other business organizations nestle is also working hard in this challenging time to run its business operations successfully. For nestle it is even more difficult because it is a multinational company and operating its business in 189 countries.
Nestle is a well-recognized brand and known as one of the world’s largest food
 and beverages selling company. Nestle is operating its business in 189 different countries with about 352000 employees working in different parts of the world. Nestle deals in selling of 2000 brands of beverages and snack but their main products are ice cream, powder and liquid beverages and pet care products. In spite of this huge setup nestle faced many challenges during this time.
The sales volume and revenues decreased as compared to the previous years and the company suffered loss. Like every business nestle is in difficult position
1

            4.00   3.00
     because of the pandemic & inflation and it is even more difficult for it because they are running their business globally.
There were many reasons behind this loss but main problem was the pandemic. After doing analysis of the business, it has been analysed that nestle is facing
 many problems like increase in the cost of raw material, scarcity of drinking water, loss due to recalling and destruction of products, growing competition and presence of multiple competitors in market
Accordingly, it is required to develop an integrated strategic plan and business plan for Nestle to help the company operate successfully in this challenge time and address its problems, provided that this plan includes - for example, but not limited to - the following aspects:
1) Vision and mission
2) The external environment assessment
3) The internal environment Assessment
4) Quadratic Analysis (SWOT / TOWS / QSPM)
5) The most important strategies that you can be used
6) Strategic objectives in the next phase
7) The internal policies to be followed to implement the proposed strategies
8) Evaluation and control mechanisms that can be used
9) Business plan includes: Marketing, financial and operational plan

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
1) Vision and mission: Nestle’s vision should be to provide high-quality food and beverage products to people all around the world, while also contributing to the sustainable development of the planet. Its mission should be to become the world’s leading food and beverage company by innovating, increasing efficiencies, and providing value to its customers.

2) The external environment assessment: Nestle should conduct a thorough analysis of the external environment, taking into consideration factors such as the pandemic, inflation, competition, and changing customer preferences. This analysis will help the company identify opportunities and threats that it can leverage or mitigate, respectively.

3) The internal environment assessment: The internal environment assessment should cover an analysis of the company’s strengths and weaknesses, including its workforce, financials, operations, and culture. This will help Nestle identify areas where it can leverage its strengths and address weaknesses.

4) Quadratic Analysis: Nestle should use SWOT (Strengths, Weaknesses, Opportunities, and Threats) analysis to determine its current position in the market and identify opportunities for improvement. This can be followed by TOWS (Threats, Opportunities, Weaknesses, and Strengths) and QSPM (Quantitative Strategic Planning Matrix) analysis to develop a suitable strategy.

5) The most important strategies that can be used: Nestle should focus on innovation and agility to respond quickly to changing customer preferences and market trends. It should also focus on strengthening its supply chain, reducing costs, and expanding its online presence.

6) Strategic objectives in the next phase: Nestle should set clear objectives for the short-term and long-term. Short-term objectives could include ensuring the availability of products at affordable prices, while long-term objectives could include expanding the company’s product line and global presence.

7) The internal policies to be followed to implement the proposed strategies: Nestle should establish clear policies to implement the proposed strategies. This may include policies related to R&D, procurement, supply chain, and marketing.

8) Evaluation and control mechanisms: Nestle should develop mechanisms to evaluate the effectiveness of its new strategies and policies. This could include monitoring key performance indicators and conducting regular audits.

9) Business plan: Nestle should develop a comprehensive business plan that includes marketing, financial, and operational plans. The marketing plan should focus on customer segmentation, product positioning, and pricing. The financial plan should focus on cost reduction and revenue maximization, while the operational plan should focus on supply chain optimization and efficiency.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
Sure, here are some more details for each of the aspects:

1) Vision and mission:
Nestle's vision statement should be specific and compelling, appealing to both customers and employees. It should clearly articulate the company's aspirations and long-term goals. Likewise, the mission statement should define the company's purpose, values, and approach to meeting the needs of its stakeholders.

2) External environment assessment:
The external environment assessment should identify the key market trends, economic conditions, and other factors that will affect Nestle's ability to compete effectively. This involves analyzing the impact of factors such as the pandemic, inflation, and competition on the company's operations.

3) Internal environment assessment:
The internal environment assessment will help Nestle identify its strengths and weaknesses, based on an analysis of its financial, operational, and cultural metrics. This will help the company assess its capacity to address new challenges, prepare for future growth, and remain competitive.

4) Quadratic Analysis:
SWOT analysis will help Nestle identify its internal and external strengths, weaknesses, opportunities, and threats. This information will be used to develop a plan of action to address the issues highlighted by the analysis. TOWS analysis will help the company define strategies based on the connections between its strengths, weaknesses, opportunities, and threats. QSPM analysis will help Nestle assess the feasibility and relative value of alternative strategies.

5) The most important strategies that can be used:
To remain competitive, Nestle needs to prioritize several strategies. Establishing a culture of innovation focused on anticipating customer needs and delivering new and improved products will be important in unlocking new markets, and retaining current ones. Additionally, streamlining the company's supply chain, reducing costs, and leveraging technology will help to optimize Nestle's operations and reduce its risk exposure.

6) Strategic objectives in the next phase:
Setting clear and measurable objectives is crucial in developing a strategic plan. These objectives should be established for the short-term and long-term. Short-term objectives could include increasing revenue volume or reducing unnecessary expenses, while long-term objectives could include expanding the company's global presence or introducing new product lines.

7) The internal policies to be followed to implement the proposed strategies:
A set of clear and actionable policies and procedures must be developed to implement the proposed strategies effectively. For instance, policies could cover investment in R&D, procurement of raw materials, supply-chain management, marketing, and sales.

8) Evaluation and control mechanisms:
Nestle must establish a system to evaluate and monitor the effectiveness of its strategic plan. The metrics used must be meaningful, measurable, and time-bound. They could include indicators such as revenue growth, customer satisfaction, inventory levels, and employee engagement.

9) Business plan:
In addition to the strategic framework, a well-crafted business plan is necessary to ensure the implementation of the proposed strategic plan. The business plan should align the company's goals and objectives with its operational activities. It should cover areas such as identifying new markets, product development, staffing, financial management, operational goals, and analysis of market trends.
